decks.notes: The main strategy of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures, such as Griselbrand and Ashen Rider, using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead. Cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual help accelerate the process, while disruptive spells like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize are used to control the opponent's hand. The deck is focused on generating a strong board presence early on and overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures, making it a strong combo-reanimator deck in the Legacy format.

This control deck aims to use a combination of strong removal spells, counter magic, and card advantage engines to outvalue the opponent and control the game. The deck includes efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, as well as powerful counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation. Card filtering and advantage are provided by Brainstorm, Ponder, and Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath. The deck also includes a companion in Yorion, Sky Nomad for additional value. Overall, the deck's key strategy is to establish control of the game and outlast the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Eldrazi Temple and Ancient Tomb to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer early in the game. The deck also utilizes disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Cavern of Souls to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive Eldrazi creatures like Eldrazi Linebreaker and Glaring Fleshraker. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells in order to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on building a strong board presence by deploying disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and using cards like Swords to Plowshares to control the opponent's threats. It also utilizes mana denial strategies with cards like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter to slow down the opponent. Stoneforge Mystic and equipment like Batterskull provide additional threats and resilience. The deck includes cards like Yorion, Sky Nomad for additional value and resilience in longer games. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive creatures.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly deploy threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and land destruction. By utilizing cheap cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm, along with efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo throughout the game. Cards like Daze and Force of Will provide protection for your threats, while Wasteland deals with problematic lands. Overall, this deck aims to be aggressive and proactive while also having the tools to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through disruption and resource denial by utilizing cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Wasteland. It also utilizes the graveyard as a resource with cards like Life from the Loam and Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis. The deck aims to grind out value over the long game while also generating threats with cards like Grist, the Hunger Tide and Eumidian Hatchery. The deck's mana base is built around utility lands like Karakas and Urza's Saga, and it has tools to deal with specific threats like Boseiju, Who Endures and Nihil Spellbomb.

The main focus of this Legacy 1 vs 1 deck is to generate mana quickly with cards like Seething Song, Desperate Ritual, and Ruby Medallion in order to cast powerful spells like Burning Wish and Storm of Memories. By utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage and Jeska's Will to draw cards and generate additional mana, the deck aims to create explosive turns that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The inclusion of cards like Bonus Round and Flashback provide additional versatility and utility to the deck, allowing for multiple lines of play to achieve victory.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping into powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also utilizes the combo potential of Omniscience and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to overwhelm the opponent. With a mix of countermagic like Force of Will and Flusterstorm, as well as card selection tools like Ponder and Brainstorm, the deck aims to disrupt and overpower the opponent's game plan while setting up its own game-winning plays.

The deck focuses on maintaining control of the board through efficient removal spells and counter magic while also utilizing cards like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler to apply pressure and quickly finish off the opponent. With a low mana curve and a focus on tempo plays, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and quickly close out games before they have a chance to stabilize. By utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, the deck seeks to consistently outpace and outmaneuver its opponents.

This deck focuses on playing cheap, efficient spells and creatures to maintain tempo and apply pressure to the opponent. By using cards like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler to quickly establish a board presence, combined with disruption from cards like Force of Will and Wasteland, the deck aims to stay ahead on the board and in card advantage. The key strategy is to keep the opponent on the back foot while steadily building towards a win condition, such as Murktide Regent or burn spells like Lightning Bolt.

This Dimir Tempo deck aims to control the pace of the game by disrupting the opponent with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf. The deck's land destruction elements, such as Wasteland, further hinder the opponent's ability to develop their game plan. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and additional disruption, allowing the deck to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory through a combination of tempo and aggression.

This Dimir Tempo deck aims to disrupt the opponent's plays using hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and counter spells like Force of Will, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and efficient creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. The deck also focuses on tempo plays with cards like Daze and Flow State, and utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student for card advantage and control. Wasteland and Undercity Sewers help to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while Mishra's Bauble provides card selection. This deck seeks to apply pressure while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on maintaining control of the board while applying pressure with efficient creatures and disruption spells. By utilizing a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and versatile creatures like Snapcaster Mage and Brazen Borrower, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win through incremental advantage. The inclusion of cards like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters allows for a variety of threats to be presented, forcing the opponent to answer multiple angles of attack. Overall, the deck seeks to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory through tempo advantage.

The deck focuses on quickly getting out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier by using the combo of Show and Tell and Omniscience. It includes card draw and disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo and control the game. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats that can bypass traditional casting costs. The deck aims to win the game in a few explosive turns by ramping into big threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the opponent's board while applying pressure. Using cards like Swords to Plowshares and Skyclave Apparition to remove threats, and Stoneforge Mystic to search for equipment like Solitude and Meteor Sword to enhance your creatures. The deck also utilizes Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion for additional value and card advantage. With a focus on efficient creature removal and strong equipment, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining a strong board presence.

This UR Tempo deck aims to disrupt the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Force of Will while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. Ponder, Brainstorm, and Preordain help filter through the deck to find the right answers and threats. Cards like Lightning Bolt and Daze provide additional removal and disruption, while Mishra's Bauble and Cori-Steel Cutter offer card advantage and flexibility. The deck's goal is to establish an early advantage and maintain tempo throughout the game by combining aggressive plays with disruptive elements.
